title: Company Overview Assessment - Set 2
description: Assessment covering company history, culture, products, values, certifications, leadership, manufacturing facilities and workplace practices.

questions.question: What does JSL primarily manufacture?
questions.explanation: JSL is a stainless steel manufacturing company.

questions.question: Which factor contributes most to customer satisfaction?
questions.explanation: Quality products improve customer satisfaction.

questions.question: Which department is responsible for employee welfare?
questions.explanation: Human Resources manages employee welfare.

questions.question: Which of these best describes workplace ethics?
questions.explanation: Ethics involve honesty, responsibility and integrity.

questions.question: Continuous improvement mainly focuses on:
questions.explanation: Continuous improvement is an ongoing effort to improve products and processes.

questions.question: Why are company policies important?
questions.explanation: Policies help employees follow standard procedures.

questions.question: Which of the following best represents teamwork?
options: Departments collaborating to achieve common goals
questions.correctAnswer: Departments collaborating to achieve common goals
questions.explanation: Teamwork improves efficiency and productivity.

questions.question: Which document introduces new employees to company rules?
questions.explanation: The handbook explains company policies and procedures.

questions.question: Which of these supports a positive workplace culture?
questions.explanation: Respectful collaboration strengthens workplace culture.

questions.question: Which area is most important during employee onboarding?
questions.explanation: Onboarding familiarizes employees with the organization.

questions.question: Safety rules should be followed only inside production areas.
questions.explanation: Safety applies across the entire workplace.

questions.question: Quality is everyone's responsibility.
questions.explanation: Every employee contributes to quality.

questions.question: Ignoring SOPs can increase operational risks.
questions.explanation: Standard procedures reduce errors and accidents.

questions.question: Company values are only relevant for senior management.
questions.explanation: Values apply to every employee.

questions.question: Customer satisfaction is an important business objective.
questions.explanation: Meeting customer expectations is a key goal.

questions.question: Why is teamwork important in an organization?
questions.sampleAnswer: Teamwork improves collaboration, communication and productivity.

questions.question: Name two company values that employees should follow.
questions.sampleAnswer: Examples include Integrity, Safety, Quality, Respect and Teamwork.

questions.question: Why is quality important for customers?
questions.sampleAnswer: Quality ensures reliable products and improves customer satisfaction.

questions.question: What is the purpose of employee onboarding?
questions.sampleAnswer: It helps employees understand the company, workplace and policies.

questions.question: Name any two major departments in a manufacturing company.
Which department ensures products meet specifications?
questions.explanation: Quality departments inspect and verify products.

questions.question: Why are certifications valuable for a manufacturing company?
options: They demonstrate compliance with recognized standards.
questions.correctAnswer: They demonstrate compliance with recognized standards.
questions.explanation: Certifications build customer confidence.

questions.question: Which behaviour should every employee demonstrate?
questions.explanation: Professional behaviour supports organizational success.

questions.question: What is one benefit of following Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs)?
questions.explanation: SOPs help standardize work and reduce errors.

questions.question: What should an employee do after noticing an unsafe condition?
questions.explanation: Unsafe conditions should always be reported promptly.
